behavior: A user can inspect aggregate local storage health and explicitly reclaim SQLite WAL and already-free pages without deleting application records, exposing private details, using credentials, or requiring connectivity.
revision_scope: The clean committed offline storage inspection and cleanup implementation within active Milestone 3.

commands.command: env -u JOBSENTINEL_LIVE_KEYRING_TESTS cargo test -p jobsentinel-storage --lib connection::tests::maintenance_tests -- --nocapture
commands.relevant_result: All 7 focused tests passed for aggregate-only inspection, production incremental-vacuum configuration, physical reclamation, row preservation, integrity and foreign-key checks, success and busy provenance, in-memory use, non-file WAL refusal, and databases without incremental vacuum.
commands.caveat: Live keyring tests were explicitly disabled. The storage library test owner uses a fixed test-only SQLCipher key and did not prompt for Keychain access.

commands.command: env -u JOBSENTINEL_LIVE_KEYRING_TESTS cargo clippy -p jobsentinel-storage -p jobsentinel-application --lib -- -D warnings
commands.relevant_result: The changed storage and application production libraries passed Clippy with warnings denied.
commands.caveat: An exploratory all-target Clippy command also linted legacy tests and failed on 1,718 existing unwrap, expect, and float-comparison findings. Those unrelated tests were not changed.

commands.relevant_result: Migrations 0 through 12 applied to an isolated database and SQLx offline metadata remained current for every workspace owner.
commands.caveat: No migration or prepared-query metadata change was needed for the SQLite PRAGMA-based maintenance contract.

commands.command: npm run harness:check; npm run lint:file-size; npm run lint:docs; npm run lint:deps; npm run lint:architecture; npm run lint:security
commands.relevant_result: Harness, hard file limits, documentation, dependency pins, architecture ownership, security sensors, and secret scanning passed on the final diff.
commands.caveat: Three changed owners reached the modularization review threshold without exceeding hard limits. Repository GitHub Actions remain temporarily disabled by user direction.

commands.command: env -u JOBSENTINEL_LIVE_KEYRING_TESTS cargo fmt --all -- --check; git diff --check
commands.relevant_result: Rust formatting and patch whitespace passed on the final implementation.
commands.caveat: The full Rust workspace test lane was not run while the user was away because downstream on-disk tests can enter macOS credential paths. Focused storage tests and production-library Clippy were used instead.

commands.command: Two read-only adversarial reviews for privacy, data loss, Rust, SQLx, and SQLite behavior
commands.relevant_result: Both reviewers returned PASS after fixes for new-database incremental-vacuum activation, vacuum and checkpoint ordering, fresh WAL reporting, and non-file WAL coverage.
commands.caveat: The reviewers inspected the final files without running Tauri, live-keyring, or signed-package paths.

decisions.inspection: Return only typed health, aggregate reclaimable bytes, optional WAL bytes, incremental-vacuum availability, and a false connectivity requirement. Do not return paths, table counts, record contents, or credentials.
decisions.refusal: Run quick and foreign-key checks before any cleanup provenance or maintenance mutation. Require compatible backup recovery when either check fails.
decisions.cleanup: Use checked SQLite TRUNCATE checkpoints and at most 100 pages of native incremental vacuum. Do not add a generic deletion, vacuum, reindex, FTS rebuild, artifact cleanup, or repair service.
decisions.configuration: Enable incremental auto-vacuum in encrypted connection options before new schemas are created. Do not force a full VACUUM on existing databases that report auto-vacuum disabled.
decisions.ordering: Checkpoint before maintenance, vacuum already-free pages, verify integrity, record success, checkpoint again, and only then return a fresh report. Convert success provenance to failed if finalization fails.
decisions.privacy: Keep cleanup fully local and offline. Do not call credential, Keychain, AI, network, source, model, backup, export, restore, or package owners.
decisions.credential_tests: Keep live keyring tests opt-in. Every unattended Rust command unsets JOBSENTINEL_LIVE_KEYRING_TESTS.

review.adversarial_verdict: PASS after SQLite activation, ordering, reporting, and non-file WAL corrections
remaining_findings: The renderer, Tauri command surface, and Settings review UI remain later Milestone 3 work.
Existing databases created without incremental auto-vacuum still receive checked WAL cleanup but do not claim page reclamation. Enabling it would require a separate reviewed full-VACUUM design.
The success-to-failed provenance conversion for a reader appearing only at the second checkpoint is not directly regression-tested.
Run filesystem behavior on Windows 11, macOS 26, and Linux, including removable and network filesystems, in the release matrix.
Milestone 3 Privacy Doctor, support bundle, policy, consent, offline repair, and platform-health work remain active.
